title: ceropteris chrysosora
intro: Ceropteris chrysosora, also known as the Golden Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ceropteris chrysosora include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Ceropteris chrysosora are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and teas, which are believed to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
